items.title: Amid slump, Ohtani stars again on mound in win
items.summary: Out of the lineup again as he slumps at the plate, Shohei Ohtani continued his dominance on the mound Wednesday night, striking out eight in seven scoreless innings in a 4-0 win over the Giants.
items.author: Alden Gonzalez

items.title: Braves rally by Cubs, first in majors to 30 wins
items.summary: The Braves improved MLB's top record to 30-13 with another thrilling win Wednesday night, scoring three runs in the eighth inning to beat the Cubs 4-1.

items.title: Misiorowski exits another gem due to cramping
items.summary: Brewers starter Jacob Misiorowski dominated the Padres for seven innings before leaving with a cramp in his right quadriceps, the second time in three starts that he has left due to cramping.

items.title: Rockies' McCarthy turns rare unassisted DP for OF
items.summary: Jake McCarthy recorded the first unassisted double play by an outfielder in the big leagues since 2015, accomplishing the feat for the Colorado Rockies against the Pittsburgh Pirates on Wednesday.

items.title: Soto fouls ball off right foot, later exits for Me...
items.summary: Mets manager Carlos Mendoza said X-rays came back negative on superstar Juan Soto, who was removed from Wednesday night's 3-2, extra-inning win over the Detroit Tigers after fouling a ball off his right foot.
items.author: Jorge Castillo

items.title: Fan at White Sox game injured in fall into bullpen...
items.summary: The Royals-White Sox game at Rate Field was delayed for a brief time Wednesday after a fan fell into the Kansas City bullpen in right field.
items.author: Bradford Doolittle

items.title: Yanks' Fried has sore elbow, set to undergo tests
items.summary: Yankees ace Max Fried was pulled from Wednesday's start against the Orioles after just three innings because of soreness in his pitching elbow.

items.title: Mets' Alvarez tears meniscus as IL woes continue
items.summary: Francisco Alvarez will undergo surgery for a meniscus tear in his right knee, with Mets manager Carlos Mendoza hoping his catcher can return in six to eight weeks.

items.author: Doc Holliday
